---
title: "Evaluation Guide"
---

# Automated Evaluation with LangSmith

This guide covers how to run automated quality tests on the Tenant First Aid
chatbot with LangSmith — dataset management, evaluation runs, and interpreting the
scores.

::: {.callout-tip}
Using Claude Code? Type `/evaluation` in the Claude Code UI for guided
evaluation-workflow assistance.
:::

Start with [Overview](getting-started/overview.qmd) and
[Definitions](getting-started/definitions.qmd) — they're short and shared by
all audiences. Then follow the path for your role.

## Legal / Content Contributor (Browser)

No terminal needed. Requires a LangSmith Plus-tier seat and access to the Cloud
deployment.

1. [Testing with Cloud Studio](legal-contributor/cloud-studio.qmd) — chat
   with the agent and iterate on the system prompt in your browser.
2. [Editing the System Prompt](editing-and-results/system-prompt.qmd) —
   change the chatbot's tone, rules, and legal guardrails.
3. [Editing Evaluator Rubrics](editing-and-results/evaluator-rubrics.qmd) —
   change what counts as a good answer.
4. [Adding or Editing Examples in the Browser](legal-contributor/examples-in-browser.qmd)
   — add test questions and reference answers in LangSmith.
5. [Running Experiments from the UI](legal-contributor/bound-evaluators.qmd)
   — start a scoring run without writing code.
6. [Viewing & Comparing Results](editing-and-results/viewing-results.qmd) —
   read the scores and the judge's rationale.

## Frontend / Content Contributor (Local)

You're comfortable with a terminal and a text editor but don't need to touch
Python or LangChain internals.

1. [Local Studio with `langgraph dev`](content-contributor/local-studio.qmd)
   — spin up the full agent locally without a LangSmith account.
2. [Editing the System Prompt](editing-and-results/system-prompt.qmd) — edit
   `system_prompt.md` directly and see it take effect immediately.
3. [Editing Evaluator Rubrics](editing-and-results/evaluator-rubrics.qmd) —
   edit `evaluators/*.md` in your code editor.
4. [Contributing Test Examples](content-contributor/contributing-examples.qmd)
   — add prompt-attack scenarios or edge cases without Python knowledge.
5. [Viewing & Comparing Results](editing-and-results/viewing-results.qmd) —
   read the scores in LangSmith.

## Backend Contributor

You'll use the full dataset CLI and run evaluations programmatically.

1. [Setup & Environment Variables](backend-contributor/setup-and-environment.qmd)
   — configure your local environment.
2. [Dataset Management (CLI)](backend-contributor/dataset-management.qmd) —
   push, pull, validate, diff.
3. [Running Evaluations (CLI)](backend-contributor/running-evaluations.qmd) —
   execute the full test suite.
4. [Understanding Scores](backend-contributor/understanding-scores.qmd) —
   calibrate evaluators and interpret results.
5. [Typical Workflows](backend-contributor/workflows.qmd) — common patterns
   and step-by-step guides.
6. [Troubleshooting](backend-contributor/troubleshooting.qmd) — fix things
   when they break.

## Related documentation

- [Developer Guide](../developer-guide/index.qmd) — code organization and system design.
- [Roadmap](roadmap/roadmap.qmd) — what's done and what's planned.
